rulings.Case Title: KATE TALBOT VS. NARASIMHA RAO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion For Summary Adjudication Against Cross Defendants Narasimha Rao And Ashapurna Ghosh
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 29, 2020 line 1. CROSS COMPLAINANT HENRY TANG's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT READ THIS NOTIFICATION IN ITS ENTIRETY. Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Cross-Complainant's Motion for Summary Judgment is OFF CALENDAR. Two Declarations supporting this motion were filed and served (electronically and by mail) on April 14, 2020, i.e. did not provide for sufficient notice (75 days plus service). Furthermore, declarations filed on April 14, 2020 indicate a hearing date of June 11, 2020, which would provide even shorter notice than the accurate hearing date of June 29, 2020. No indication on file that opposing parties have been notified of June 29, 2020 hearing date. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: DANIEL L'ABBE VS. AERON NOWELL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Aeron Nowell To Respond To Discovery Impose Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 29, 2020 line 2. PLAINTIFF DANIEL L'ABBE's Motion To Compel Defendant Aeron Nowell To Respond To Discovery Impose Sanctions READ THIS NOTIFICATION IN ITS ENTIRETY. Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Discovery Responses is DENIED. Moving party fails to cite any authority for the proposition that a motion to compel discovery may be granted as to defaulted defendants. = (501/CFH)
